version_history_page_description: Control how many versions are kept per asset. Versions beyond the limit are soft-deleted during the nightly cleanup and permanently purged after 7 days.
version_history_settins_only_owners: Only workspace owners can change version history settings.
keep_all_versions_description: No limit, every uploaded version is preserved indefinitely.
keep_n_versions: Keep last N versions per asset
keep_n_versions_description: Versions beyond the limit are removed during the nightly cleanup.
tab_exif_privacy_description: Control how photo metadata is extracted and stored. Changes apply to future uploads; use the backfill button to process existing assets.
keep_gps_description: By default, GPS location data is stripped to protect privacy. Enable only if your workflow requires location-tagged assets. Applies to future ingests; use backfill to re-extract existing assets.
extract_exif_again_description:
This will queue a background job for every image asset that hasn't had EXIF extracted yet.
      The process runs in the background and may take a few minutes for large libraries.
no_rules_yet: No rules yet. Rules filter or route incoming files.

ingress_own_mail_desc: Zero config, get a unique ingest address

ingress_s3_desc: AWS S3, R2, MinIO, Backblaze B2…

ingress_mail_help_attachments:
Send files as email attachments to this address.
Supported formats: images, video, PDF, and common creative files.
Max 25 MB per file.

member_invite_description: Manage your workspace members and their permissions.

delete_ingress_source_message: This will also remove all log entries for this source.
audio_extract_description: Rip the audio from this video into a standalone audio file.
audio_transcode_description: Re-encode this audio file in a different format or quality.
audio_normalize_description: Adjust perceived loudness to a consistent level using EBU R128.
audio_normalize_explainer: EBU R128 normalization adjusts perceived loudness to a consistent level. -16 LUFS is standard for most streaming platforms.
hp_meta_description: Damask is a self-hosted digital asset management system for creative teams that want full control over their files and metadata.
hp_hero_note: Runs on your hardware. No usage caps. No per-seat tiers.
hp_problem_text: Cloud DAM tools work until they don't: the pricing jumps, the contract ends, or the upload quota runs out. And even when they work, your files sit on someone else's server, behind a third-party login, in a format you didn't choose.
hp_problem_answer: Damask runs on your machine or your server. Your storage, your rules, your backups.
hp_who_solo_body: Damask replaces a folder structure that stopped making sense two years ago. Fast upload, instant search, and a clean way to find the right version of anything.
hp_who_teams_body: Designers, photographers, video editors working on shared projects. Roles and permissions keep work organized without locking people out. Everyone sees what they need, nothing more.
hp_who_studios_body: Client deliverables, brand assets, production libraries. Damask tracks who uploaded what, when, and what changed. Share work with clients without giving them an account. Keep the audit trail in-house.
hp_features_sub: Not a simplified version of it. The full thing.
hp_feat_library_1: Drag-and-drop upload, multiple files at once
hp_feat_library_3: List and grid views, compact or spaced
hp_feat_library_4: Full-text search across filenames and custom fields
hp_feat_library_5: Filter by type, project, folder, tag, date, or custom field
hp_feat_library_6: EXIF extracted on ingest, GPS stripped by default
hp_feat_org_1: Projects: group by client, campaign, or shoot
hp_feat_org_2: Folders: two-level depth, drag assets between them
hp_feat_org_3: Tags: workspace-scoped, auto-created on first use
hp_feat_org_5: Custom fields: text, number, date, single/multi-select
hp_feat_variants_1: Non-destructive: the original is never touched
hp_feat_variants_3: Manual crop or smart content-aware crop
hp_feat_variants_6: Video transcoding, frame capture, audio normalization
hp_feat_versioning_1: Upload a new file: previous version is preserved
hp_feat_versioning_2: One-click rollback with full ownership history
hp_feat_versioning_3: Share links always point to the current version
hp_feat_sharing_2: Password protection and expiry dates on any share
hp_feat_sharing_3: Client review mode: view and comment without a Damask account
hp_feat_ingress_1: Email ingest: attach files, route via subaddressing
hp_feat_ingress_3: Google Drive and Canva OAuth connections
hp_feat_ingress_4: Pull on a schedule or trigger via webhook
hp_feat_audit_1: Every metadata change leaves a record
hp_feat_audit_2: Renames, retags, moves, uploads, restores, share
hp_feat_access_3: OIDC login: Keycloak, Authelia, Authentik, Okta, Auth0
hp_tech_body: A single Go binary. Few free external runtime dependencies: FFmpeg, ImageMagick. Runs on Linux, macOS, or a cheap VPS. SQLite by default: the storage layer is an interface, swap in S3-compatible storage in config, no code changes.
hp_selfhost_step1_body:
A Linux server or local machine, Docker or a compiled binary, and a storage path.
That's it.
hp_selfhost_step2_body: All files. All metadata. All audit history. No usage caps, no per-seat pricing tiers, no data retention clauses.
hp_selfhost_step3_body: Schema migrations run at startup. No manual steps. No downtime window. No migration scripts to run by hand.
hp_diag_headline: One asset. Every format. Every destination.
hp_diag_subline: Connect your sources, define your variants, share with whoever needs them.
hp_diag_legend_ingress: Ingress files pulled from any source
errors_system_tag_protected: This operation is not allowed on system tags
errors_no_watermark_asset: No asset tagged _watermark found in this workspace
bulk_validation_empty: Add at least one tag or field to apply.
workspace_settings_locked_taxonomy_descr: When enabled, editors and viewers can only apply tags from the existing tag library. New tags can only be created by owners in Settings → Tags.
workspace_settings_locked_taxonomy_confi: All {count} existing tags become the controlled vocabulary.
tag_not_in_vocabulary_hint: Ask a workspace owner to add it to the tag library.
tag_vocab_locked_api_error: This tag is not in the vocabulary. Ask a workspace owner to add it.

settings_account_password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
settings_account_delete_description: Permanently deletes your account and removes access to every workspace. This cannot be undone.
